dtcs.description: Theft Deterrent System Communication Line High Fixation If the communication line (EFIO-IMI) to the transponder key ECU assembly is stuck high output (e.g. shorted to +B), the ECM stores this DTC. NOTICE: * If the transponder key ECU assembly is replaced, register the key and ECU communication ID See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * If the ECM is replaced, register the ECU communication ID See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT If any DTCs other than DTC B279A are output, troubleshoot those DTCs first. Result B --  GO TO DTC CHART  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Engine Immobilizer System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU - ECM) (a) Disconnect the transponder key ECU assembly connector. (b) Disconnect the ECM connector. (c) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY (OUTPUT) (a) Reconnect the transponder key ECU assembly connector. (b) Reconnect the ECM connector. (c) Using an oscilloscope, check the waveform. Waveform 1 (Reference) Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the transponder key ECU assembly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
REGISTER KEY (a) Register the key    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REGISTER ECU COMMUNICATION ID (a) Register the ECU communication ID    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: 31 - Center Airbag Sensor Assembly Malfunction The center airbag sensor assembly consists of a deceleration sensor, safing sensor, drive circuit, diagnosis circuit, ignition control, etc. If the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the deceleration sensor, it determines whether or not the SRS should be activated.

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result NOTICE: If only DTC B1000/31 is output, do not clear the DTCs, replace the center airbag sensor assembly. HINT If any DTCs other than DTC B1000/31 are also output, troubleshoot those DTCs first. B --  GO TO DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Airbag System A --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Short to B+ in Door System Communication Bus Malfunction

Short to GND in Door System Communication Bus Malfunction * When a +B short circuit or body ground short circuit is detected on the communication bus (BEAN), BEAN is disabled and a DTC is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE (BACK DOOR ECU) (a) Disconnect the back door ECU connector and check for DTCs B1214 and B1215. OK: Neither DTC B1214 nor DTC B1215 is output. NOTICE: Disconnect the connector in the operational sequence, and start the next step after the connector is reconnected. OK --  REPLACE BACK DOOR ECU NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MAIN BODY ECU - BACK DOOR ECU) (a) Check for a short-circuit in B+ or body ground. (b) Disconnect the S2 back door ECU connector and E6 main body ECU connector. (1) Measure the voltage between the terminals of the S2 back door ECU or E6 main body ECU connector and body ground. Standard voltage: (2) Check the resistance. Standard resistance: (c) Reconnect the back door ECU connector and main body ECU connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE WIRE HARNESS BETWEEN MAIN BODY ECU AND BACK DOOR ECU OK --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU

dtcs.description: is output when communication between the back door ECU and the main body ECU stops for more than 10 seconds. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK COMMUNICATION LINE (a) Disconnect the E6 main body ECU and S2 back door ECU connectors. (b) Check the resistance. Standard resistance: (c) Reconnect the main body ECU and back door ECU connectors. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK BACK DOOR ECU (POWER SOURCE) (a) Disconnect the S2 back door ECU connector. (b) Measure the voltage between the terminals of the S2 back door ECU connector and body ground. Standard voltage: (c) Reconnect the ECU connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK BACK DOOR ECU (GROUND) (a) Disconnect the S2 back door ECU connector. (b) Check the resistance. Standard resistance: (c) Reconnect the ECU connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE BACK DOOR ECU

dtcs.description: Fuel Sender Open Detected This DTC is output when the combination meter assembly detects a fuel sender gauge malfunction. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (FUEL METER OPERATION) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter following menus: Body Electrical / Combination Meter / Data List. (e) According to the display on the Techstream, perform the Active Test. Combination Meter OK: Fuel receiver gauge indication is normal. NG --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Combination Meter/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FUEL INPUT) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter following menus: Body Electrical / Combination Meter / Data List. (e) According to the display on the Techstream, read the Data List. Combination Meter OK: Fuel value displayed on the tester is approximately the same as needle indication. NG --  INSPECT FUEL SENDER GAUGE ASSEMBLY OK --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Combination Meter/Removal

INSPECT FUEL SENDER GAUGE ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the fuel sender gauge assembly    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Fuel Gauge Sender/Service and Repair/Removal. Text in Illustration (b) Check that the float moves smoothly between E and F. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Reinstall the fuel sender gauge assembly. NG --  REPLACE FUEL SENDER GAUGE ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Fuel Gauge Sender/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FUEL SUCTION PLATE SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the fuel suction plate sub-assembly    See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration (c) Reinstall the fuel suction plate sub-assembly. NG --  REPLACE FUEL SUCTION PLATE SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - FUEL SUCTION PLATE SUB-ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the E13 combination meter assembly connector. (b) Disconnect the L5 fuel suction plate sub-assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Reconnect the fuel suction plate sub-assembly connector. (e) Reconnect the combination meter assembly connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Combination Meter/Removal

Disconnect the connector from the front seat side airbag assembly LH.

dtcs.description: 44 - Roll Over Cut Off Indicator Malfunction The curtain airbag cutoff switch is a mechanism that operates both the right and left side of the curtain shield airbag and the front seat outer belt when the center airbag sensor detects a rollover. The RSCA OFF indicator is installed in the combination meter. If the curtain airbag cutoff switch is operated, the indicator comes on to inform the driver that the rollover detection system is not working. The initial setting of the rollover detection system is on. It automatically operates every time the ignition switch is turned ON. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK RSCA OFF INDICATOR OPERATION (a) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (b) Check the RSCA OFF indicator operation. Result B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor assembly and the combination meter assembly. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor assembly and the combination meter assembly. (b) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor assembly side and combination meter assembly side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY (RSCA OFF INDICATOR) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (f) Check the RSCA OFF indicator operation. Result B --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY A -- Continue to next step.

(b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Combination Meter/Removal

NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Disconnect the connector from the combination meter assembly. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: (f) Turn the ignition switch off. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER SOURCE) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE POWER SOURCE CIRCUIT OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY (RSCA OFF INDICATOR) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the connector to the combination meter assembly. (d) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Using a service wire, connect E13-21 (ES) and E13-19 (R/O) of the combination meter assembly connector. (g) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (h) Check the RSCA OFF indicator operation. OK: RSCA OFF indicator comes on. NG --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Combination Meter/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor assembly. (d) Connect the connector to the combination meter assembly. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch to ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (h) Turn the ignition switch off. (i) Turn the ignition switch to ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (j) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the passenger side buckle switch circuit. Troubleshoot DTC B1771 first when DTCs B1771 and B1795 are output simultaneously. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. Holding the seat for a long period of time may cause a problem, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (b) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the occupant classification ECU and the front seat inner belt assembly RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO B+) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the occupant classification ECU and the front seat inner belt assembly RH. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (d) Measure the voltage. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR OPEN) (a) Turn the ignition switch to the lock position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Using a service wire, connect Q3-2 and Q3-1 of connector C. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting. (d) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (FOR SHORT) (a) Disconnect the service wire from connector C. (b) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.
CHECK NO. 1 SEAT WIRE (TO GROUND) (a) Measure the resistance. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 1 SEAT WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Connect the connectors to the occupant classification ECU and the front seat inner belt assembly RH. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (d) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the lock position. REPLACE FRONT SEAT INNER BELT ASSEMBLY RH (a) Turn the ignition switch to the lock position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the front seat inner belt assembly RH    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Inner Belt Assembly/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle if possible. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (e) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (f) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor assembly. (g) Turn the ignition switch to the lock position. (h) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (i) Check the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch to the lock position. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the occupant classification ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Occupant Classification Module - Air Bag/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (b) Connect a Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to the on position. (d) Using the Techstream, perform the zero point calibration    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. OK: COMPLETED is displayed on the tester. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using a Techstream, perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. (1) Confirm that nothing is placed on the passenger seat. (2) Confirm that the beginning sensor reading is within the standard range. Standard range: -3.2 to 3.2 kg (-7 to 7 lb) (3) Place a 30 kg (66.14 lb) weight (e.g. a lead mass) onto the front passenger seat. (4) Confirm that the sensitivity is within the standard range. Standard range: 27 to 33 kg (59.52 to 72.75 lb) HINT When performing the sensitivity check, use a solid metal weight (the check result may not be accurate if a liquid weight is used). NEXT --  END

HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front passenger seat installation bolts to see the under surface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not tip over. dtcs.description: Push Switch / Key Unlock Warning Switch Malfunction This DTC will be output if the transponder key ECU does not detect that the unlock warning switch is ON even when the ignition switch is in the ON position. Under normal conditions, the unlock warning switch is ON when the ignition switch is in the ON position. NOTICE: If the transponder key ECU is replaced, register the key and ECU communication ID. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Connect a Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (c) Turn the tester ON. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Immobiliser / Data List. (e) According to the display on tester, read the "Data List". Immobiliser: OK: ON (Key is in ignition key cylinder) appears on the screen. NG --  INSPECT UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal
INSPECT UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH (a) Remove the unlock warning switch    See: Body and Frame/Locks/Power Locks/Power Door Lock Switch/Service and Repair/Unlock

Standard resistance: (c) Reconnect the unlock warning switch connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

(c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (d) Reconnect the transponder key ECU connector. (e) Reconnect the unlock warning switch connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal

Replace the key if the key code registration is not performed normally and this DTC is detected. NOTICE: If the key is replaced, register the key. PROCEDURE

(b) Re-register the key    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

NG --  CHECK KEYS OK --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK KEYS (a) Insert the vehicle's other key into the ignition key cylinder. (b) Check that the engine starts with this key. OK: The engine starts. NG --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER OK --  RE-REGISTER OR REPLACE KEY THAT CANNOT START ENGINE

REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER (a) Replace the transponder key amplifier    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK ENGINE START (a) Check that the engine starts. OK: The engine starts. NG --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END (TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER IS DEFECTIVE)

dtcs.description: Communication Malfunction No. 1 This DTC is output when an error occurs in communication between the transponder key amplifier and the transponder key ECU. HINT Some noise is found in the communication line. NOTICE: If the transponder key ECU assembly is replaced, register the key and ECU communication ID. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK KEYS (a) Check whether the ignition key being used is near other ignition keys, as shown in the illustration. Also, check whether the key ring is in contact with the key grip. Result: B --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Separate the keys from each other, or remove the key ring. (b) Delete the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Insert a key into the ignition key cylinder and remove the key. Repeat for all the other keys of the vehicle. OK: No code is output. NG --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER OK --  END

REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER (a) Replace the transponder key amplifier    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm System Transmitter/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK ENGINE START (a) Check that the engine starts. OK: The engine starts. NG --  REPLACE TRANSPONDER KEY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END (TRANSPONDER KEY AMPLIFIER IS DEFECTIVE)

dtcs.description: Engine Immobiliser System Malfunction This DTC is output when one of the following occurs: 1) the ECM detected errors in its own communications with the transponder key ECU; 2) the ECM detects errors in the communication lines; or 3) the ECU communication ID between the transponder key ECU and ECM is different and an engine start is attempted. Before troubleshooting for this DTC, make sure no transponder key ECU DTCs are present. If present, troubleshoot the transponder key ECU DTCs first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Delete the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTC. OK:

ECU CODE REGISTRATION (a) Re-register the ECU communication ID between the transponder key ECU and ECM    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Delete the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTC. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the ECM and the transponder key ECU. OK: Connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSPONDER KEY ECU - ECM) (a) Disconnect the E79 ECU and E77 ECM connectors. (1) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.
REPLACE ECM (a) Replace the ECM    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

ECU CODE REGISTRATION (a) Re-register the ECU communication ID between the transponder key ECU assembly and ECM    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
